title: Encryption Function
description: After her computer class Sophie invented her own encrypting function, which takes a number as an input. Given a number it treats it as a sequence of base-10 digits (with no leading zeroes), masks out every possible subset of positions in this sequence, interprets the new sequence as a base-$10$ number (possibly with leading zeroes) and adds all numbers obtained in such a way. So far Sophie failed to devise a decryption algorithm. Help her--write a program that decrypts the encrypted number.
input: Input consists of a single positive integer $n$ ($1 \le n \le 10^{18}$), this is the output of Sophie's encryption function.
output:
In the first and only line of the output you should write a single positive integer $m$, for which the encrypted value is $n$, or NIE (Polish for 'no') if no such a number exists.

If there are several correct answers, you can output any of them.
hint:
In Sample 1, computing the value of the encryption function on $123$ gives $1 + 2 + 3 + 12 + 13 + 23 + 123 = 177$.

In Sample 2, there is no sequence whose encrypted value is $42$.
source: ICPC, Regionals, Europe, Central European Regional Contest, Poland Collegiate Programming Contest, AMPPZ 2017
